summary: CC-0090 ships a well-structured, fail-closed, opt-in chart-level NetworkPolicy that hardens the keystone-operator pod itself, with complete REQ-001..REQ-013 traceability, strict JSON schema, helm-unittest coverage, a Chainsaw E2E test on kind, and reference + how-to documentation. The code matches project conventions (selectorLabels pinning, {{ fail }} defensive guards, opt-in defaults). One BLOCKING finding: the back-link decision block in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md still contains the leftover 'Reviewer: please verify this placement matches intent.' phrase, which matches the external-feedback pattern 'resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge' (CC-0065) — sourcery-ai specifically flagged this phrasing on the CC-0065 merge and it must be removed before merge. Two minor suggestions: the DNS selector schema accepts empty maps (which would match all namespaces/pods as a NetworkPolicyPeer), and the IPv4 CIDR regex is lenient on octet bounds.

pass: REQ-001 default-off posture covered: tests/networkpolicy_test.yaml asserts hasDocuments:0 with default values
REQ-002 default-deny verified: test 'default-deny both directions' asserts policyTypes contains Ingress and Egress with no catch-all rule
REQ-003 DNS egress rule verified: test asserts UDP 53 + TCP 53 with configured kube-system/kube-dns selectors
REQ-004 single-rule N×M kube-apiserver verified: test 'emits a single egress rule with all CIDRs and all ports' asserts one egress entry containing multiple to[] and multiple ports[]
REQ-006 fail-closed guards verified: two failedTemplate tests cover empty cidrs and empty ports at render time with the exact error message wording
REQ-007 webhook ingress fallback verified: test covers both the explicit webhookClients.cidrs path and the fallback-to-kubeApiServer.cidrs path
REQ-008 metrics ingress opt-in verified: test with empty allowMetricsFrom asserts zero metrics ingress rules; populated allowMetricsFrom test asserts peer is rendered verbatim
REQ-009 no 8081 rule verified: test 'does not render ingress rule for probe port 8081' asserts absence

REQ-005 schema validation coverage added to tests/schema_validation_test.yaml: invalid CIDR, out-of-range port, non-boolean enabled, additionalProperties rejection, empty list rejection when enabled=true

REQ-010/REQ-011 Chainsaw E2E at tests/e2e/keystone-operator/network-policy-egress/chainsaw-test.yaml installs the operator with networkPolicy.enabled=true on kind, asserts NetworkPolicy exists, applies a minimal Keystone CR, and waits for Ready=True under the restricted egress
E2E catch blocks dump operator logs (current+previous), NetworkPolicy YAML, events, and Job logs for diagnostic observability (REQ-011)
Template named templates/networkpolicy.yaml matches project convention
Comment-first preamble in networkpolicy.yaml explains default-deny, each rule's purpose, and the 8081 omission with a link to the upstream 'what you can't do' doc

Fail-closed guards mirror the defensive pattern at operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go:61-63 (open-empty-ports / empty-CIDR refusal) — consistent style across the operator
Selector pinning uses include "keystone-operator.selectorLabels" rather than hardcoding labels, so the policy follows fullnameOverride / nameOverride changes automatically
Values file documents each key inline with CC-0090 REQ-IDs, discovery command, and kind-default hints
JSON schema uses strict additionalProperties:false throughout the networkPolicy subtree, preventing silent typos like kubeAPIServer vs kubeApiServer
fail: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:27 contains 'Reviewer: please verify this placement matches intent.' — a leftover reviewer-directed note that matches the external-feedback pattern and must be converted to a plain DECISION/NOTE comment

pass: Default-off posture (networkPolicy.enabled=false) preserves upgrade safety on clusters without NetworkPolicy-enforcing CNIs (kindnet, bare Flannel) and makes the harden-up an explicit opt-in (REQ-001)
Ingress policyTypes included alongside Egress — default-denies inbound traffic to the operator pod, not just outbound (REQ-002)
Metrics port is default-denied; allowMetricsFrom requires explicit peer opt-in rather than a blanket allow (REQ-008)
Webhook ingress is scoped to API-server CIDRs rather than a world-open rule, and the fallback behaviour is documented

Fail-closed guards at template render time prevent the two known accidental-open-policy footguns (empty CIDRs → no destinations; empty ports → all ports open per NetworkPolicy semantics) even if schema validation is bypassed with --skip-schema-validation

Clear scope boundary between CC-0039 (per-CR NetworkPolicy, protects Keystone API pods) and CC-0090 (chart-level NetworkPolicy, protects the operator pod itself) — called out in both the reference doc and the how-to
Single NetworkPolicy object per release rather than split objects, matching REQ-004 and keeping the resource count minimal

Webhook client fallback (webhookClients.cidrs → kubeApiServer.cidrs when empty) is the correct default for standard clusters (API server is the sole webhook caller) with a documented escape hatch for front-ended API servers
Template uses toYaml + nindent for dns.namespaceSelector / dns.podSelector / allowMetricsFrom rather than duplicating map-rendering logic
Single egress rule for kube-apiserver (exploiting cartesian-product NetworkPolicy semantics) instead of nested loops emitting N×M rules — minimal YAML, matches REQ-004
No premature abstractions: fallback logic is a three-line if-block, not a helper template
Template {{ fail }} with specific error messages referencing CC-0090 REQ-006 on empty cidrs or empty ports — clear diagnostics at helm render time
Schema conditional allOf enforces minItems:1 on cidrs and ports when enabled=true — caught before the template runs in the default path
E2E test catch blocks dump logs/events/NetworkPolicy YAML so a failure in CI yields enough signal to debug without re-running locally
Defense-in-depth: schema validation (first line) + template fail guard (backstop) on the same invariant — correctly called out in the reference doc
Fail-closed semantics: the template refuses to render rather than rendering an unsafe policy
Documentation explicitly warns that kindnet does NOT enforce NetworkPolicy, so operators on kind understand the policy is inert without a supported CNI
fail: networkPolicy.dns.namespaceSelector and podSelector are typed as stringMap with no minProperties constraint — an empty map renders a NetworkPolicyPeer that matches all namespaces / all pods, broadening DNS egress beyond the intended kube-dns target. Minor because the happy path populates defaults, but the schema does not prevent an operator from explicitly setting {} and getting a wider policy than they expect.

issues_found.title: Leftover 'Reviewer: please verify' note in DECISION comment (CC-0090 back-link placement)
issues_found.evidence:
<!--
  DECISION (CC-0090, REQ-012): back-link placement
  Ambiguity: The task specified "operator hardening section", but this document
  has no section by that name — it is structured by sub-reconciler contract, not
  by security topic. The top-of-doc link block is the single location every
  reader hits, so a pointer here reliably surfaces the operator-pod NetworkPolicy
  reference for readers researching operator-side hardening. A second in-line
  back-link also appears in the `reconcileNetworkPolicy` sub-condition row below
  to clarify the CC-0039 (per-CR) vs CC-0090 (operator-pod) distinction.
  Reviewer: please verify this placement matches intent.
-->
issues_found.rationale: This is the exact external-feedback pattern flagged by sourcery-ai on CC-0065 ('In buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy the inline comment still contains Reviewer: please verify.; consider removing this leftover note now that the behavior is codified and covered by tests'). The documented fix in .planwerk/review_patterns/resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge.md is to convert reviewer-directed language into a plain DECISION/NOTE comment now that the behaviour is codified and tested. The back-link has been shipped and validated end-to-end; there is no longer an open question.
issues_found.fix: Remove the 'Reviewer: please verify this placement matches intent.' line from docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:27. Keep the rest of the DECISION block — it is a valid rationale comment that documents why the back-link sits at the top rather than under a section called 'operator hardening'.

issues_found.title: networkPolicy.dns selector stringMap has no minProperties — empty map silently broadens DNS egress
issues_found.location: operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.schema.json:26-32 (stringMap definition referenced from dns.namespaceSelector / dns.podSelector at lines 217-226)
issues_found.rationale: If an operator explicitly sets networkPolicy.dns.namespaceSelector: {} or podSelector: {}, Kubernetes interprets an empty matchLabels as matching every namespace / every pod. The rendered NetworkPolicyPeer would then permit UDP/TCP 53 egress to every pod in the cluster, not just kube-dns. This is broader than intended by the reference doc's rule table and silently undermines the defense-in-depth goal of the policy. The default values populate the selectors correctly, so the happy path is safe, but the schema does not catch a misconfigured override.
issues_found.fix: Either add minProperties: 1 to the stringMap definition (tightens everywhere it is used), or add minProperties: 1 locally on dns.namespaceSelector / dns.podSelector in the networkPolicy.dns subtree. Preferred: local constraint on the two dns selector properties, to avoid perturbing unrelated stringMap consumers. Add a schema_validation_test_yaml test case that asserts the schema rejects {} for these fields.

issues_found.title: IPv4 CIDR regex accepts octets > 255 (e.g., 999.999.999.999/32)
issues_found.rationale: The IPv4 branch accepts any 1-3 digit group, so '999.999.999.999/32' passes schema validation. The Kubernetes API server will reject the malformed CIDR at admission time, so cluster state never becomes corrupted — but the error surface shifts from helm-render (clear schema message) to kubectl apply (opaque admission error). Low impact because the fail-closed layer catches it before reaching the cluster, but the schema could be tighter.
issues_found.fix: Replace the IPv4 half of the pattern with a strict per-octet form, e.g. '((25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9][0-9]?)\\.){3}(25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|[01]?[0-9][0-9]?)/([0-9]|[12][0-9]|3[0-2])'. Before applying, confirm the project prefers strict per-octet validation over the simpler lenient form — other projects accept the lenient form for readability.

suggested_improvements.title: Add helm-unittest case for schema rejection of empty DNS selectors
suggested_improvements.rationale: Pairs with finding CC-0090-R1-002. Once minProperties:1 is added, a regression test keeps the guard honest.

suggested_improvements.title: Cross-reference E2E test from docs/reference/keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md#testing
suggested_improvements.rationale: Reference doc already lists the Chainsaw path correctly; no change required. Noted as verified, not a fix.

next_steps: Remove 'Reviewer: please verify this placement matches intent.' from docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:27. The enclosing DECISION block should remain (it is a legitimate rationale comment); only the reviewer-directed closing sentence needs to go.

Add minProperties: 1 on networkPolicy.dns.namespaceSelector and networkPolicy.dns.podSelector (or on the shared stringMap definition) in values.schema.json, plus a corresponding rejection test in tests/schema_validation_test.yaml.
Optional: tighten the IPv4 half of the cidr regex to enforce per-octet bounds — ask the team before changing, since the current lenient form is a deliberate trade-off in some codebases.

After the two AUTO_FIX items above are applied, this feature is ready to merge. All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-013) have passing tests, the fail-closed guards mirror the established project pattern, and documentation is complete (reference + how-to + sidebar integration).

mandatory_patterns_matched.evidence: networkPolicy.enabled defaults to false in operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml:46 with CC-0090 REQ-001/REQ-005 comment explaining rationale

mandatory_patterns_matched.name: requirement-id-traceability-across-code-tests-docs
mandatory_patterns_matched.evidence: REQ-001..REQ-013 tags appear in template comments, values.yaml comments, schema descriptions, helm-unittest test names, Chainsaw test comments, and both reference and how-to docs

external_feedback_patterns_matched.name: resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge
external_feedback_patterns_matched.match: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:27 contains 'Reviewer: please verify this placement matches intent.' — identical phrasing to the sourcery-ai feedback on CC-0065 that created this review pattern. The pattern exists specifically to catch this recurring leftover.
external_feedback_patterns_matched.action: Remove the phrase; keep the surrounding DECISION block.
